rulings.Case Title: LOU ANN BASSAN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs And (Real Party In Interest Leeann Phelham, In Her Official Capacity As Executive Director Of San Francisco Ethics Commission)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 10, 2019, Line 1, RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO ETHICS COMMISSION's Motion To Tax Costs And (Real Party In Interest Leeann Phelham, In Her Official Capacity As Executive Director Of San Francisco Ethics Commission) Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to tax costs is granted as to all costs sought to be stricken and plaintiff Lou Ann Bassan's request in her opposition paper for an award of attorney's fees is denied. $1,110.55 of Ms. Bassan's claimed costs are taxed. While Ms. Bassan's error in initially filing her writ in the Court of Appeal is understandable, her incurrence of the Court of Appeal filing fee was not reasonably necessary per the costs statutes case law. Ms. Bassan's costs for exhibits and court reporter transcripts are not allowable per the costs statutes. Treating Ms. Bassan's request for fees as a properly filed cross-motion, the request is denied because Ms. Bassan has made no showing that her litigation success conferred a benefit on anyone other than herself. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the City is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN NEALAN VS. LEDCOR CONSTRUCTION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendant Ledcor Construction, Inc.S To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Further Responses To (1) Supplemental Interrogatories And (2) Supplemental Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 10, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT LEDCOR CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S Motion By Defendant Ledcor Construction, Inc.S To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Further Responses To (1) Supplemental Interrogatories And (2) Supplemental Request For Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued to January 23, 2019 on the court's motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to arlene.barton@dentons.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ZACHARY BOOKMAN ET AL VS. FRANK NOLAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Zachary Bookman, Darian Shirazi Motions To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admissions, Set One (Tentative Ruling Purposes Part 2 Of 2)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 10, 2019, Line 3, Plaintiffs Zachary Bookman, Darian Shirazi'S Motions To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admissions, Set One (Tentative Ruling Purposes Part 2 Of 2) The motion is granted in part as to form interrogatories 17.1 as set forth below: The court orders that any General Objections included in the form interrogatories must be removed. With respect to Defendant's response to form interrogatory 17.1, the responses are limited to the first 35 request for admissions based on plaintiffs' failure to provide a declaration for additional discovery when more than 35 request for admissions are propounded. Cal. Civ. Proc. Code . § 2033.040(a). 1(c) - Provide the full contact information for Peter Blitz. 6(b)-(d): Respond to all parts. Or provide an argument as to why you cannot provide facts, witnesses or documents. 12 (a)-(d): Respond to all parts. Or provide an argument as to why you cannot admit or deny or provide facts, witnesses or documents. 16 (a)-(d): Respond to all parts. Or provide an argument as to why you cannot admit or deny or provide facts, witnesses or documents. 20 (a)-(d): Respond to all parts. Or provide an argument as to why you cannot admit or deny or provide facts, witnesses or documents. 21 (a)-(d): Respond to all parts. Or provide an argument as to why you cannot admit or deny or provide facts, witnesses or documents. 24 (a)-(d): Respond to all parts. Or provide an argument as to why you cannot admit or deny or provide facts, witnesses or documents. 29(a)-(d): Respond to all parts. Or provide an argument as to why you cannot admit or deny or provide facts, witnesses or documents. 31(a)-(d): Respond to all parts. 32(a)-(d): Respond to all parts. Limit it to criminal activity by the alleged "gang leader." 33(a)-(d): Respond to all parts. Limit it to criminal activity by the alleged "gang leader." 34(a)-(d): Respond to all parts. Limit it to criminal activity by the alleged "gang leader." 35(a)-(d): Respond to all parts. Limit it to criminal activity by the alleged "gang leader." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to arlene.barton@dentons.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (End of part 2 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ZACHARY BOOKMAN ET AL VS. FRANK NOLAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motions To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admissions, Set One
rulings.Rulings: PLAINTIFF ZACHARY BOOKMAN, DARIAN SHIRAZI Motions To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admissions, Set One (Part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiffs Zachary Bookman and Darian Shirazi's motion regarding compelling further responses from Defendants to special interrogatories and requests for admission are denied as untimely. The motion compelling further responses to form interrogatories other than 17.1 are denied as untimely and 17.1 is limited as set forth below. It is a basic tenant of the Discovery Act that in order to compel further responses to interrogatories, compel further responses to requests for admission, or compel a further response to an inspection demand, the movant must notice the respective motions "within 45 days of the service of the verified response, or any supplemental verified response, or on or before any specific later date to which the propounding party and the responding party have agreed in writing." Cal. Civ. Proc. Code § 2030.300(c) (interrogatories); see Cal. Civ. Proc. Code § 2031.310(c) (demand for inspection of documents); Cal. Civ. Proc. Code § 2033.290(c) (requests for admission). Failure to timely notice said motions results in waiver. Id. §§ 2030.300(c), 2031.310(c), 2033.290(c). The email correspondence reflects that the extension of time to bring a motion to compel was only given in reference to form interrogatory 17.1. Defense counsel's emails dated September 19, 2018 and October 22, 2018 clearly state the extensions were only as to Form Interrogatory 17.1 and that they did not agree to any other extensions. (end of part 1 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JUSTIN AROWCAVAGE VS. VENUE BOY ENTERTAINMENT, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 10, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT VENUE BOY ENTERTAINMENT, LLC's Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default Defendant Venue Boy Entertainment, LLC's motion to set aside the default entered against it on July 10, 2018 is granted. Venue Boy has made a sufficient showing of surprise, inadvertence, mistake and excusable neglect entitling it to have the default set aside per the discretionary relief provisions of CCP 473(b). Granting the motion comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits, especially where, as here, plaintiff Justin Arowcavage has not identified any legal prejudice he would suffer by granting the motion. Mr. Arowcavage's request for fees and costs is denied in the exercise of the court's discretion since, in the court's view, Mr. Arowcavage should have agreed to defendants' request that the defaults be set aside by stipulation since it was apparent that defendants desired to litigate this case and were, at least in some fashion, engaging in settlement efforts at or near the time the defaults were entered. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: SKANSKA USA CIVIL WEST CALIFORNIA DISTRICT, INC., VS. WEBCOR BUILDERS/OBAYASHI CORP., A JOINT VENTURE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 10, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANT TRANSBAY JOINT POWERS AUTHORITY DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Transbay Joint Powers Authority's demurrer to the sixth cause of action for declaratory relief in the second amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Although not raised in the demurrer, the sixth cause of action is likely invalid due to the failure of plaintiff Skanska USA Civil West California District, Inc. to file a government claim against TJPA because the sixth cause of action, fairly construed, seeks release of retained funds. (See Canova v. Trustees of Imperial Irrigation District Employee Pension Plan (2007) 150 Cal. App. 4th 1487, 1493 (government claim required for a mandamus claim where the primary purpose of the claim is recovery of money or damages)). In all events, because Skanska has adequate remedies against Webcor, the declaratory relief claim is neither necessary nor proper at this time per CCP 1061. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for TJPA is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk
